title: elaeocarpus robustus var. collinus
intro: Elaeocarpus robustus var. collinus is a plant species commonly known as the 'Mountain Blue Quarrendine' or 'Blue Olive Berry'. It is a shrub or small tree native to Australia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant's leaves, bark, and fruit have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in topical applications, such as a poultice or salve, to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
